title: Bambleshwari Temple
tagline: Bambleshwari Temple — spiritual town of Chhattisgarh

overview.short: Bambleshwari Temple is a Hindu temple, located at Dongargarh in Rajnandgaon district, Chhattisgarh, India.
overview.description: Bambleshwari Temple is a Hindu temple, located at Dongargarh in Rajnandgaon district, Chhattisgarh, India. It is on a hilltop of 1600 feet. This temple is referred to as Badi Bambleshwari. Another temple at ground level, the Chhotti Bambleshwari is situated about 1/2 km from the main temple complex.

topPlaces.name: Eastern States Union
topPlaces.description: The Eastern States Union was a short-lived (1947–48) union of princely states in newly independent India that gathered most of the princely states of the former Orissa Tributary States and Chhattisgarh States Agency in order to fill the vacuum of power created after the departure of the British and the wrapping up of the British Raj.

topPlaces.name: Samta Colony
topPlaces.description: Samta Colony is an residential area situated in the heart of Raipur, Chhattisgarh, India. It is surrounded by Choubey Colony.

topPlaces.name: Mahamaya Temple, Raipur
topPlaces.description: Shree Rajrajeshwari Maa Mahamaya Devi Mandir Raipur or popularly Mahamaya Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Devi Mahamaya, located at Purani Basti, Raipur, Chhattisgarh. The temple is one of the 36 Shaktipeeths or 36 forts of Chhattisgarh.

topPlaces.name: Balbir Singh Juneja Indoor Stadium
topPlaces.description: Balbir Singh Juneja Indoor Stadium is an Indoor stadium in Raipur, Chhattisgarh. The stadium is only indoor stadium in city and was named after Balbir Singh Juneja who was born in the city with capacity of 4,000.

topPlaces.name: Raipur
topPlaces.description: Raipur (Hindi: rāypura, pronounced [ɾaːɪpʊɾᵊ] ) is the capital city of the Indian state of Chhattisgarh. Raipur is also the administrative headquarters of Raipur district and Raipur division, and the most populous city of the state.

topPlaces.name: Raipur division
topPlaces.description: Raipur division is an administrative unit of the Indian state of Chhattisgarh. Headquartered in the capital city of Raipur, it is the administrative, financial, and educational nerve center of the state.

topPlaces.name: Vivekananda Sarovar, Raipur
topPlaces.description: Vivekananda Sarovar, popularly called Swami Vivekananda Sarovar or Budha Talab, is a lake in Raipur, the capital city of Chhattisgarh, India. It is located in Budhapara area near the Balbir Singh Juneja Indoor Stadium and it is spread in 30 hectares.

topPlaces.name: Dudhadhari temple
topPlaces.description: Dudhadhari temple is a Hindu temple, located at Raipur Mathpara in the Indian state of Chhattisgarh. The current Head Priest (Mahant) of the temple is Rajeshri Dr.

items.note: The Eastern States Union was a short-lived (1947–48) union of princely states in newly independent India that gathered most of the princely states of the former Orissa Tributary States and Chhattisgarh States Agency in order to fill the vacuum of power created after the departure of the British and the wrapping up of the British Raj.
Samta Colony is an residential area situated in the heart of Raipur, Chhattisgarh, India. It is surrounded by Choubey Colony.

Shree Rajrajeshwari Maa Mahamaya Devi Mandir Raipur or popularly Mahamaya Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Devi Mahamaya, located at Purani Basti, Raipur, Chhattisgarh. The temple is one of the 36 Shaktipeeths or 36 forts of Chhattisgarh.

itinerary.title: Day 2 — Balbir Singh Juneja Indoor Stadium & around

items.note: Balbir Singh Juneja Indoor Stadium is an Indoor stadium in Raipur, Chhattisgarh. The stadium is only indoor stadium in city and was named after Balbir Singh Juneja who was born in the city with capacity of 4,000.

Raipur (Hindi: rāypura, pronounced [ɾaːɪpʊɾᵊ] ) is the capital city of the Indian state of Chhattisgarh. Raipur is also the administrative headquarters of Raipur district and Raipur division, and the most populous city of the state.

Raipur division is an administrative unit of the Indian state of Chhattisgarh. Headquartered in the capital city of Raipur, it is the administrative, financial, and educational nerve center of the state.

Vivekananda Sarovar, popularly called Swami Vivekananda Sarovar or Budha Talab, is a lake in Raipur, the capital city of Chhattisgarh, India. It is located in Budhapara area near the Balbir Singh Juneja Indoor Stadium and it is spread in 30 hectares.
Dudhadhari temple is a Hindu temple, located at Raipur Mathpara in the Indian state of Chhattisgarh. The current Head Priest (Mahant) of the temple is Rajeshri Dr.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Bambleshwari Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Bambleshwari Temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Bambleshwari Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Swami Vivekananda Airport, Raipur (15 km) and the nearest railway station is Raipur Junction (3 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Bambleshwari Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Bambleshwari Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Bambleshwari Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Bambleshwari Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Bambleshwari Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Eastern States Union, Samta Colony, Mahamaya Temple, Raipur, Balbir Singh Juneja Indoor Stadium, Raipur.
